pull.title: kernel: reset header validation state before processing
pull.body:
This PR updates validation-state docs to consistently describe output states as overwritten in-place.

It also fixes `btck_chainstate_manager_process_block_header` to reset its validation state before processing, preventing reused states from keeping a previous failure after a successful header check.

A regression test is added for the reused-state case.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/35189. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Stale ACK | [sedited](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35189#pullrequestreview-4210479084), [alexanderwiederin](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35189#issuecomment-4428313450), [yuvicc](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35189#issuecomment-4440586623) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#33856](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33856) (kernel: Refactor process_block_header to return btck_BlockValidationState by yuvicc) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first.
I wonder what the better pattern is here. Should we always be resetting them?

> Should we always be resetting them?

With the current public kernel API wrappers, yes. `ValidationState` is only updated on failure in many validation paths; a successful check can just return true without rewriting the state. So if a caller reuses a state object after a failed check, the old `INVALID` result could otherwise carry over into a later successful call.

events.message:
kernel: reset header validation state before processing

Reset the state supplied to
btck_chainstate_manager_process_block_header before calling
ProcessNewBlockHeaders. ProcessNewBlockHeaders can return
success without rewriting a reused state object, so callers could otherwise
observe a previous failure after a successful header check.

Document the public transaction and block validation state out-parameters as
overwritten in-place, matching the reset-on-entry behavior, and add a
regression test for the reused-state case.

events.body:
Updated the PR to address the review feedback.

The `btck_block_check` docs now use the shorter “Overwritten in-place...” wording. I also fixed the inconsistent `btck_chainstate_manager_process_block_header` case: its `block_validation_state` was documented as an out-param, but the wrapper did not reset it before calling `ProcessNewBlockHeaders`, so a reused state could retain a previous failure after a later successful header check.

The wrapper now resets that state on entry, the public docs describe the overwrite behavior consistently, and a regression test covers the reused-state case.

rename.to: kernel: reset header validation state before processing

🐙 This pull request conflicts with the target branch and [needs rebase](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#rebasing-changes).

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -1303,9 +1303,11 @@ typedef uint32_t btck_BlockCheckFlags;
  *                                  optional POW and merkle-root checks. Use
  *                                  btck_BlockCheckFlags_BASE to run only the base
  *                                  checks.
- * @param[in,out] validation_state  Non-null, previously created with
- *                                  btck_block_validation_state_create and updated
- *                                  in-place with the validation result.
+ * @param[out]    validation_state  Non-null, previously created with
+ *                                  btck_block_validation_state_create. Reset on
+ *                                  entry (any prior contents are overwritten) and
+ *                                  updated in-place with the validation result
comments.body: Doesn't "updated in-place" imply that any value is overwritten? I think it would be better to only document the out-params that are *not* always updated in-place and overwritten. (or rather, try to always update them and overwrite them, where possible)

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -1303,9 +1303,11 @@ typedef uint32_t btck_BlockCheckFlags;
  *                                  optional POW and merkle-root checks. Use
  *                                  btck_BlockCheckFlags_BASE to run only the base
  *                                  checks.
- * @param[in,out] validation_state  Non-null, previously created with
- *                                  btck_block_validation_state_create and updated
- *                                  in-place with the validation result.
+ * @param[out]    validation_state  Non-null, previously created with
+ *                                  btck_block_validation_state_create. Reset on
+ *                                  entry (any prior contents are overwritten) and
+ *                                  updated in-place with the validation result
comments.body:
I agree that updated in-place can imply this to someone familiar with the validation-state pattern, but I think it is still worth spelling out here because this is public kernel API documentation.

From a caller’s point of view, “updated in-place” mainly says that the object passed in is mutated. It does not necessarily make it obvious that any previous result is cleared before the check runs. Since callers may reuse the same `btck_BlockValidationState` across calls, documenting the reset-on-entry behavior makes the contract explicit and avoids them having to know how the internal `ValidationState` paths behave.

  *                                  optional POW and merkle-root checks. Use

  *                                  checks.
- * @param[in,out] validation_state  Non-null, previously created with
- *                                  btck_block_validation_state_create and updated
- *                                  in-place with the validation result.
+ * @param[out]    validation_state  Non-null, previously created with
+ *                                  btck_block_validation_state_create. Reset on
+ *                                  entry (any prior contents are overwritten) and
+ *                                  updated in-place with the validation result
comments.body:
Ok, but I think briefly saying `Overwritten in-place with the ...` is fine, if `updated in-place` is too ambiguous and could mean that it is not reset.

Also, what I wanted to say that this should be consistent, and it seems more important to look at the cases where it is *not* overwritten. Currently there is:

```
 * @param[out] block_validation_state   The result of the btck_BlockHeader validation.
```

So this looks like a bug that should be fixed there.
